name: Right Deltoid
description: Large triangular shoulder muscle that caps the glenohumeral joint and gives the shoulder its rounded contour.
origin: Lateral third of the clavicle, the acromion, and the spine of the scapula.
action: Abducts the arm (middle fibres); anterior fibres flex and medially rotate, posterior fibres extend and laterally rotate the arm.
clinical: Axillary nerve injury (surgical-neck fracture or shoulder dislocation) paralyses the deltoid, causing loss of arm abduction and a flattened shoulder.
